name: Aktiv Grotesk
designer: Bruno Maag, Sahar Afshar, Alex Blattmann, Pilar Cano, Fernando Caro, Emanuela Conidi, Kalapi Gajjar, Pablo Gámez, Fabio Haag, Michele Patanè, Jonny Pinhorn, Diogo Rapazote, Samir Reis, José Solé, Spike Spondike, Andrés Torresi
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Aktiv Grotesk as a grotesque sans typeface with Weight, Width and Italic variable font axes, designed and developed 2010 to 2025. The page lists asymmetrical terminal cuts, slanted bottom terminals, even proportions, contemporary grotesque shaping, monolinear modulation and moderately closed apertures.
summary: A grotesque sans workhorse with Weight, Width and Italic variable axes and support for ten global writing systems.
description: Dalton Maag's Aktiv Grotesk is a grotesque sans typeface offered as a 3-axis variable font with Hairline to Black weights, Condensed to Extended widths and matching italics, totalling 54 static styles. Drawn over 2010 to 2025 by Bruno Maag with a large team including Sahar Afshar, Pilar Cano, Emanuela Conidi, Fabio Haag and Michele Patanè, it covers Arabic, Armenian, Cyrillic, Devanagari, Georgian, Greek, Hebrew, Latin, Latin Extended, Malayalam and Thai across more than 1,380 languages. The page describes asymmetrical terminal cuts, slanted bottom terminals, monolinear modulation and moderately closed apertures, and the family ships with an extensive icon set.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Aktiv Grotesk as a powerhouse of versatility and functionality, a grotesque sans with Weight, Width and Italic variable font axes, support for ten global writing systems and an extensive icon set, ready for even the most demanding projects.

name: Aldgate Sans
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Aldgate Sans as a modern humanist sans typeface inspired by the distinctly British designs of the early 1900s, with generous proportions for legibility and an approachable tone.
summary: A humanist sans rooted in British type of the early 1900s, paired with Aldgate Serif and offered as a Weight, Width and Italic variable font.
description: Dalton Maag's Aldgate Sans is a humanist sans by Ignacio Casco Guijarro, designed and developed in 2023 and inspired by distinctly British type of the early 1900s. The variable font carries Weight, Width and Italic axes spanning Hairline to Black across seven widths from ExtraCondensed to ExtraExtended, with matching italics. It covers Latin only with support for over 800 languages, and pairs with Aldgate Serif as a unified system.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Aldgate Sans as a modern humanist sans typeface inspired by the distinctly British designs of the early 1900s, with generous proportions ensuring legibility, an approachable tone and a variable font offering extensive Weight, Width and Italic axes for exceptional versatility.

name: Aldgate Serif
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Aldgate Serif as a contemporary serif typeface designed to support and complement its Sans cousin, with open letter shapes, a warm and dignified tone, and a variable font with extensive Weight, Width and Italic axes.
summary: A contemporary serif companion to Aldgate Sans, with Weight, Width and Italic variable axes and a warm, dignified tone.
description: Dalton Maag's Aldgate Serif is a contemporary serif by Ignacio Casco Guijarro, released 2023 to pair with Aldgate Sans. The variable font runs Hairline to Black across seven widths from ExtraCondensed to ExtraExtended with matching italics. The page describes open letter shapes that ensure legibility with a warm and dignified tone, with Latin coverage spanning over 800 languages.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Aldgate Serif as a contemporary serif typeface designed to support and complement its Sans cousin, its open letter shapes ensuring legibility with a warm and dignified tone, and its variable font offering extensive Weight, Width and Italic axes for exceptional versatility.

name: Aller
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Aller as a warm, characterful and inventive humanist sans typeface, with three text styles plus a more extreme Display cut that uses small caps for some lowercase letters.
summary: A warm humanist sans by Marc Weymann with three text weights, italics and a Display cut that swaps in small caps for impact.
description: Dalton Maag's Aller is a humanist sans by Marc Weymann, designed and developed 2010 to 2023. The family carries three text weights (Light, Regular, Bold) with matching italics and a Display style that uses small caps in place of some lowercase letters for added impact. It covers Latin with support for over 840 languages.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Aller as a warm, characterful and inventive humanist sans typeface, the family including three text styles with matching italics plus a more extreme Display style which uses small caps for some of its lowercase letters, giving the font more impact and recognition.

name: Almaq
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Almaq as a bold and punchy sans typeface with two variant styles in the same weight: Refined, a traditional approachable display, and Rough, degraded and askew.
summary: A two-variant display sans by Fabio Haag pairing a refined cut with a degraded, askew Rough sibling.
description: Dalton Maag's Almaq is a display sans by Fabio Haag, designed and developed 2011 to 2017. It ships in two variants of the same weight: Refined, framed as a traditional and approachable display font, and Rough, a degraded and askew counterpart. Latin and Latin Extended coverage supports over 860 languages.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Almaq as a bold and punchy sans typeface with two variant styles, ideal for eye-catching statements: Refined is a traditional, approachable display font, while Rough is degraded and askew.

name: Amarelle Chancery
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Amarelle Chancery as part of the Amarelle suite, inspired by Hermann Zapf's chancery of 1979, drawing on shared features to connect with its Sans and Serif companions.
summary: A chancery script in the Amarelle suite, reinterpreting Hermann Zapf's 1979 chancery alongside its Sans and Serif siblings.
description: Dalton Maag's Amarelle Chancery is a chancery script by Ben Jones, designed and developed 2025. It runs Hairline to Black with matching italics and is the third member of the Amarelle suite alongside Amarelle Sans and Amarelle Serif. OpenType features include alternate ampersand, descending k, short tail Q and swash capitals. Latin coverage supports over 790 languages.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Amarelle Chancery as part of the Amarelle suite, a cohesive trio designed to work together, with the Chancery inspired by Hermann Zapf's chancery of 1979 and reinterpreted to draw on shared features that create a natural connection with its Sans and Serif companions.

name: Amarelle Sans
designer: Alex Blattmann, Ben Jones, Diogo Rapazote
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Amarelle Sans as part of the Amarelle suite, rooted in Hermann Zapf's flared sans of 1958 and developed alongside the Serif and Chancery styles to form a unified typographic voice.
summary: A flared sans rooted in Hermann Zapf's 1958 design, drawn as the sans corner of the Amarelle suite.
description: Dalton Maag's Amarelle Sans is a flared sans by Alex Blattmann, Ben Jones and Diogo Rapazote, designed and developed 2024 to 2025. The variable font runs Hairline to Black with matching italics on an Italic axis, and forms the sans member of the Amarelle suite alongside Amarelle Serif and Amarelle Chancery. Latin coverage supports over 790 languages.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Amarelle Sans as part of the Amarelle suite, a harmonious trio of typefaces designed to complement one another, rooted in Hermann Zapf's flared sans of 1958 and developed alongside the Serif and Chancery styles to form a unified typographic voice.

name: Amarelle Serif
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Amarelle Serif as one of a unified trio of typefaces in the Amarelle suite, inspired by Hermann Zapf's influential 1948 serif design and reimagined alongside its Sans and Chancery counterparts with shared cues for stylistic cohesion.
summary: A serif inspired by Hermann Zapf's 1948 design, drawn as the serif corner of the Amarelle suite.
description: Dalton Maag's Amarelle Serif is a serif by Deiverson Ribeiro and Ben Jones, designed and developed 2024 to 2025. The variable font runs Hairline to Black with matching italics on an Italic axis, and forms the serif member of the Amarelle suite alongside Amarelle Sans and Amarelle Chancery. Latin coverage supports over 790 languages.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Amarelle Serif as one of a unified trio of typefaces that form the Amarelle suite, inspired by Hermann Zapf's influential 1948 serif design and reimagined alongside its Sans and Chancery counterparts, with shared cues that enhance stylistic cohesion.

name: Ballare
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Ballare as an elegant and fluid broad-nib script font that captures the grace and movement of dance, with flowing curves suited to display and branding.
summary: A broad-nib script in a single weight, drawn for display and branding with extensive swashes and Cyrillic alongside Latin.
description: Dalton Maag's Ballare is a broad-nib script by Lukas Paltram and Alja Herlah, designed and developed 2020. It ships in a single Regular weight with swash and swash capitals OpenType features. The page frames it as elegant, fluid and contemporary yet timeless, with Cyrillic and Latin coverage supporting over 810 languages.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Ballare as an elegant and fluid broad-nib script font that captures the grace and movement of dance, its flowing curves creating harmony, perfect for display and branding, both contemporary and timeless and bringing sophistication to diverse creative projects.

name: Bankside Sans
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Bankside Sans as an early British grotesque sans typeface reinterpreted for modern users, with clear classic letter shapes and extreme widths for impact.
summary: An early British grotesque reinterpreted for modern use, with nine widths from UltraCondensed to UltraExtended on a Weight, Width and Italic variable font.
description: Dalton Maag's Bankside Sans is a grotesque sans by Paul Troppmair and Bruno Mello, designed and developed 2023. The variable font carries Weight, Width and Italic axes, running Hairline to Black across nine widths from UltraCondensed to UltraExtended with matching italics. It pairs with Bankside Serif and covers Latin with support for over 790 languages.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Bankside Sans as an early British grotesque sans typeface reinterpreted for modern users, its clear classic letter shapes conveying intent while its extreme widths deliver impact, and its variable font providing extensive Weight, Width and Italic axes for limitless versatility.

name: Bankside Serif
designer: Paul Troppmair, Pablo Bosch, Bruno Mello
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Bankside Serif as an industrial serif typeface designed to support and complement Bankside Sans, with clear letterforms and a flexible tone of voice across extreme widths.
summary: An industrial serif companion to Bankside Sans, running nine widths from UltraCondensed to UltraExtended on a Weight, Width and Italic variable font.
description: Dalton Maag's Bankside Serif is an industrial serif by Paul Troppmair, Pablo Bosch and Bruno Mello, designed and developed 2023. The variable font carries Weight, Width and Italic axes, running Hairline to Black across nine widths from UltraCondensed to UltraExtended with matching italics. Latin coverage supports over 790 languages.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Bankside Serif as an industrial serif typeface designed to support and complement Bankside Sans, its clear letterforms giving a flexible tone of voice and its variable font having extensive Weight, extreme Width and Italic axes for remarkable versatility and impact.

name: Bisbee
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Bisbee as a swift and casual handwritten script typeface that is also highly legible and readable, with an extensive Weight axis across both Latin and Arabic.
summary: A casual handwritten script with a Weight axis from Hairline to Black across Latin and Arabic.
description: Dalton Maag's Bisbee is a casual handwritten script by Emanuela Conidi, designed and developed 2022 to 2024. The variable font carries a Weight axis from Hairline to Black across Latin and Arabic, with OpenType features including swash capitals, alternate J and j, and Arabic swashes. Coverage supports over 810 languages.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Bisbee as ready to be every designer's secret weapon, a swift and casual handwritten script typeface that is also highly legible and readable, with an extensive Weight axis across both Latin and Arabic, helping deliver any message with a friendly tone of voice.

name: Blenny
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Blenny as a fabulously curvaceous fatface display font with soft, voluptuous curves and a retro feel, suited to branding, bold headlines and product labels.
summary: A single-weight fatface display by Spike Spondike with retro curves, supporting Latin and Thai.
description: Dalton Maag's Blenny is a fatface display by Spike Spondike, designed and developed 2014 to 2020. Its single Black weight covers Latin and Thai with support for over 860 languages. OpenType features include alternate &, discretionary ligatures and a fauna icon set. The page frames it as suitable for branding, bold headlines and enticing product labels.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Blenny as a fabulously curvaceous fatface display font with soft, voluptuous curves and a retro feel, making it suitable for branding, bold headlines or enticing product labels, its single weight supporting two writing systems, Latin and Thai.

name: Bligh
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Bligh as a casual sans typeface with curvy terminals and a quirky skeleton, inspired by global voyages and adventure during the age of exploration and the industrial revolution.
summary: A three-weight casual sans by Luisa Baeta with curvy terminals and a quirky skeleton, drawn for projects that want personality.
description: Dalton Maag's Bligh is a casual sans by Luisa Baeta, designed and developed 2015 to 2017. It runs in three weights (Light, Regular, Bold) and covers Latin with support for over 750 languages. OpenType features include case-sensitive forms, fractions and an icon set. The page frames its inspiration as global voyages and adventure during the age of exploration and the industrial revolution.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Bligh as a casual sans typeface with curvy terminals and a quirky skeleton, inspired by global voyages and adventure during the age of exploration and the industrial revolution, the family being a versatile choice when you need a font that stands out from the crowd.

name: Bookman Recast
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Bookman Recast as a 21st century revival of a 19th century classic, with a wide legible design that is expressive in display and warm and friendly in text.
summary: A 21st century recast of Bookman by Emanuela Conidi and Robert Pratley, with a Weight and Italic variable font from Hairline to Black.
description: Dalton Maag's Bookman Recast is a Bookman revival by Emanuela Conidi and Robert Pratley, designed and developed 2025. The variable font runs Hairline to Black with matching italics on an Italic axis, with alternate J and Q OpenType features. Latin coverage supports over 790 languages, and the page frames it as expressive in display and warm and friendly in text.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Bookman Recast as their 21st century revival of a 19th century classic, its wide legible design making it expressive in display and warm and friendly in text, and its generous curves and thoughtful detailing giving every project a touch of charm without sacrificing readability.

name: Bressay
designer: Stuart Brown, Alex Blattmann, Matt Burvill, Jonny Pinhorn
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Bressay as an award-winning Scotch Roman serif typeface, with a compact family of two display styles and four text styles ideal for editorial use.
summary: An award-winning Scotch Roman serif with two display styles and four text styles, covering Arabic, Cyrillic, Devanagari, Greek and Latin.
description: Dalton Maag's Bressay is a Scotch Roman serif by Stuart Brown, Alex Blattmann, Matt Burvill and Jonny Pinhorn, designed and developed 2015 to 2023. It carries a TDC Typeface Design Award and ships as two display styles and four text styles with matching italics. Coverage spans Arabic, Cyrillic, Devanagari, Greek and Latin across more than 940 languages, with OpenType features including small caps, historical long s and Arabic curved quotes.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Bressay as an award-winning Scotch Roman serif typeface, exuding exquisite beauty and elegance, the compact family comprising two display styles and four text styles, ideal for editorial use and benefiting from extensive writing system support.

name: Calmetta
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Calmetta as a contemporary humanist sans with a generous x-height and slightly condensed proportions, balancing legibility and economy of space for grid-based signage and wayfinding.
summary: A humanist sans by Elí Castellanos and Marc Weymann built for signage and wayfinding, with a generous x-height held inside slightly condensed proportions.
description: A humanist sans by Elí Castellanos and Marc Weymann, drawn for grid-based signage and wayfinding where legibility has to coexist with economy of space. Four upright weights run from Light to XBold and the family ships with wayfinding icons as part of its OpenType set. Reach for it on transport, civic and infrastructure work where you want a calm, trustworthy voice that holds up at distance.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Calmetta as a contemporary humanist sans typeface with a generous x-height and slightly condensed proportions, designed by Elí Castellanos and Marc Weymann in 2017. They frame it as a useful combination of legibility and economy of space, ideal for grid-based signage and wayfinding.

name: Caraque
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Caraque as a delicious display sans inspired by melting chocolate, with Weight and Melt variable axes that flex from solid to melted shapes for playful, expressive setting.
summary: A playful display sans by Pablo Gámez with a Melt axis that runs from solid to melted, drawn for impactful, expressive setting.
description: A display sans by Pablo Gámez built around the idea of melting chocolate, with Weight and Melt variable axes that move shapes from solid to soft drips. Five static weights run from Regular to Black, and the variable font extends those into expressive states for posters, packaging and campaign work. Reach for it when you want warmth and humour in a confident, contemporary voice.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Caraque as a delicious sans typeface designed for impactful display setting across diverse media, drawn by Pablo Gámez between 2021 and 2023. They frame the variable font's Weight and Melt axes as a way to flex between humorous and down-to-earth expression.

name: Census
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Census as an evocative broad-nibbed formal script for display and era-specific text, offering a single variable italic with a Cap Height axis, swash capitals and uncial lowercase alternates.
summary: A broad-nibbed formal script by Ben Jones with a Cap Height axis, swash capitals and uncial alternates, awarded by Communication Arts and shortlisted at D&AD.
description: A broad-nibbed formal script by Ben Jones intended for display and era-specific text, with a single variable italic weight that flexes through an unusual Cap Height axis. Swash capitals and uncial lowercase alternates extend its range across editorial, packaging and identity work that wants a heritage voice without slipping into pastiche. Picked up a Communication Arts Award of Excellence and a D&AD Awards shortlist.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Census as an evocative broad-nibbed formal script that exudes elegance and sophistication, designed by Ben Jones and released in 2024. They highlight the Cap Height axis, swash caps and uncial lowercase alternates, and note recognition from Communication Arts and a D&AD Awards shortlist.

name: Co
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Co as a versatile humanist sans with an underlying geometric structure and rounded terminals, split into Text and Headline optical sizes for body and display use across multiple writing systems.
summary: A multi-script humanist sans by Ron Carpenter with optical Text and Headline cuts, built for branding and editorial work across Arabic, Cyrillic, Greek and Latin.
description: A humanist sans by Ron Carpenter that sits on a geometric skeleton and finishes with distinctive rounded terminals. Text and Headline optical sizes run across Light, Regular and Bold, so the same family flexes from long-form reading to confident display. Multi-script support across Arabic, Cyrillic, Greek and Latin Extended makes it a workhorse for global brand and editorial systems that want warmth without losing engineering.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Co as a versatile humanist sans typeface with an underlying geometric structure and unique rounded terminals, designed by Ron Carpenter between 2007 and 2021. They highlight the Text and Headline variants and the family's reach across multiple writing systems.

name: Consort Recast
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Consort Recast as a faithful revival of Stephenson Blake's 1956 mid-century slab serif, returning with extensive Weight and Width variable axes that pair vintage sturdiness with contemporary control.
summary: A faithful revival of Stephenson Blake's 1956 slab serif by Yingtong Tan, returning with Weight and Width variable axes for editorial and poster work.
description: A slab serif by Yingtong Tan that revives Stephenson Blake's 1956 Consort, the foundry's mid-century workhorse. Nine weights run from Hairline to Black across Condensed and Normal widths, and a 2-axis variable font lets you tune Weight and Width for posters, mastheads and editorial systems. The character feels period-correct without being precious; sturdy slabs, even rhythm, contemporary control.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Consort Recast as their faithful revival of Stephenson Blake's 1956 mid-century slab serif, designed by Yingtong Tan and scheduled for 2026. They frame the Weight and Width variable axes as a way to combine vintage sturdiness with contemporary control.

name: Coquito
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Coquito as a dynamic, agile script inspired by the traditional Spanish cookie, defined by fine, rapid strokes and a spontaneous, handwritten charm for packaging, headlines and social campaigns.
summary: A casual handwritten script by Pablo Bosch inspired by the traditional Spanish coquito cookie, drawn for packaging, headlines and social campaigns.
description: A handwritten script by Pablo Bosch with fine, rapid strokes and a spontaneous, unpretentious charm. A single Regular weight carries the family, supported by stylistic features for packaging, headlines and social work that want a personal, food-warm voice. Useful when a brand needs a hand on the page without the affectation of formal calligraphy.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Coquito as a dynamic, agile script font inspired by the traditional Spanish cookie, designed by Pablo Bosch and scheduled for 2026. They highlight its fine, rapid strokes and spontaneous, handwritten charm for packaging, headlines and social campaigns.

name: Cordale
designer: Fabio Haag, Ron Carpenter, Lukas Paltram
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Cordale as a versatile contemporary serif text typeface with four essential styles, intended for editorial and long-form use across print and digital and across multiple writing systems.
summary: A contemporary text serif by Fabio Haag, Ron Carpenter and Lukas Paltram with four essential styles, drawn for editorial work in print and digital across Arabic, Cyrillic, Greek and Latin.
description: A text serif by Fabio Haag, Ron Carpenter and Lukas Paltram with the four core styles needed for long-form reading: Regular, Italic, Bold and Bold Italic. The voice is calm and accessible, with multi-script support across Arabic, Cyrillic, Greek and Latin Extended that suits global publishers and brands. Pair it with a contemporary humanist sans like Foco or Effra for editorial systems that need to behave well in both print and digital.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Cordale as a versatile contemporary serif text typeface with four essential styles, designed by Fabio Haag, Ron Carpenter and Lukas Paltram between 2008 and 2021. They frame it as an excellent choice for editorial and long-form use in both print and digital formats.

name: Courier Recast
designer: Reiko Hirai, Emanuela Conidi, Jonny Pinhorn
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Courier Recast as a reinterpretation of Howard Kettler's 1950s IBM typewriter face, redrawn as a monospace variable font with Italic and an unusually extensive Weight axis.
summary: A monospace revival of Howard Kettler's IBM Courier by Reiko Hirai, Emanuela Conidi and Jonny Pinhorn, redrawn with an unusually extensive Weight axis and matching italics.
description: A monospace by Reiko Hirai, Emanuela Conidi and Jonny Pinhorn that returns to Howard Kettler's 1950s IBM typewriter face and pushes it well beyond the original. Nine weights run from Hairline to Black with matching italics, supported by a 2-axis variable font with Weight and Italic. Multi-script coverage across Cyrillic, Greek, Hebrew and Latin makes it a useful pick for editorial systems, code samples and identity work that wants the classic Courier voice with contemporary control.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Courier Recast as their reinterpretation of the classic geometric slab-serif typewriter typeface originally designed by Howard Kettler for IBM in the 1950s, reissued in 2025 by Reiko Hirai, Emanuela Conidi and Jonny Pinhorn. They highlight the Italic axis and an unusually extensive Weight axis.

name: Dark London
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Dark London as a striking display typeface with a cut-out, distressed, grungy look that borders on a ransom note style, taking Bankside Sans and Bankside Serif in a darker direction.
summary: A distressed cut-out display face by Maria Fontenelle, taking Bankside Sans and Serif into ransom note territory with a Shift variable axis.
description: A display face by Maria Fontenelle that pushes the Bankside Sans and Serif system into distressed, cut-out territory close to a ransom note. A Shift variable axis moves the letterforms from Normal to Shifted, letting designers tune how aggressive the texture feels. Reach for it on music, fashion and counter-cultural work that wants a darker, hand-cut voice without losing typographic discipline.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Dark London as a striking addition to the display typeface genre, with a cut-out, distressed, grungy look that borders on a ransom note style, designed by Maria Fontenelle and released in 2025. They frame it as a darker direction for the Bankside Sans and Bankside Serif families.

name: Darkmode
designer: Matt Burvill, Maria Fontenelle, Diogo Rapazote, Eduardo Rennó, Deiverson Ribeiro
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Darkmode as a highly legible humanist sans for modern UIs, with Weight and Italic axes and a Darkmode axis that counteracts the Irradiation Illusion to keep contrast consistent across light and dark backgrounds.
summary: A humanist sans for modern UIs by the Dalton Maag team, with a Darkmode axis that counteracts the Irradiation Illusion so contrast holds on light and dark.
description: A humanist sans by Matt Burvill, Maria Fontenelle, Diogo Rapazote, Eduardo Rennó and Deiverson Ribeiro, drawn for modern product UIs. Nine weights run from Hairline to Black with matching italics, and a 3-axis variable font adds the Darkmode axis that rebalances stroke weight against the Irradiation Illusion when type sits on dark backgrounds. Multi-script coverage across Cyrillic, Greek, Hebrew and Latin and a matching Mono make it a complete UI system for tech, fintech and product brands.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Darkmode as a highly legible humanist sans typeface for modern UIs, designed by Matt Burvill, Maria Fontenelle, Diogo Rapazote, Eduardo Rennó and Deiverson Ribeiro between 2020 and 2024. They highlight Weight and Italic axes and a Darkmode axis that counteracts the Irradiation Illusion to keep contrast consistent.

name: Darkmode Mono
designer: Eduardo Rennó, Matt Burvill, Maria Fontenelle, Diogo Rapazote, Deiverson Ribeiro
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Darkmode Mono as a monospace humanist sans with Weight and Italic axes plus a Darkmode axis that counteracts the Irradiation Illusion, holding consistency regardless of contrast between text and background.
summary: A humanist monospace by the Dalton Maag team with a Darkmode axis that counteracts the Irradiation Illusion, holding consistency between light and dark UIs.
description: A humanist monospace by Eduardo Rennó with Matt Burvill, Maria Fontenelle, Diogo Rapazote and Deiverson Ribeiro, sitting alongside the Darkmode family. Nine weights run from Hairline to Black with matching italics, and a 3-axis variable font adds a Darkmode axis that rebalances stroke weight to counteract the Irradiation Illusion in dark UI contexts. Multi-script coverage across Cyrillic, Greek, Hebrew and Latin makes it a strong pick for code, terminals and product surfaces that have to behave on light and dark.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Darkmode Mono as a monospace humanist sans typeface, designed by Eduardo Rennó with Matt Burvill, Maria Fontenelle, Diogo Rapazote and Deiverson Ribeiro between 2022 and 2024. They highlight Weight and Italic variable axes and a Darkmode axis that counteracts the Irradiation Illusion to keep contrast consistent.

name: Dedica
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Dedica as a unique and distinctive serif featuring pronounced asymmetric serifs, a compact family designed to elevate headline and display work with charm and elegance.
summary: A compact display serif by Bruno Maag with pronounced asymmetric serifs, drawn to lift headline and identity work with charm and elegance.
description: A display serif by Bruno Maag built around pronounced asymmetric serifs that give the family its signature voice. Three styles, Regular, Italic and Bold, keep the system compact while staying flexible enough for headlines, mastheads and identity systems. Useful where a brand wants warmth and elegance without slipping into pure transitional or didone territory.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Dedica as a unique and distinctive typeface featuring pronounced asymmetric serifs, designed by Bruno Maag between 2007 and 2024. They frame the compact family as one that excels in headline and display applications where its charm and elegance elevate the impact of the message.

name: Effra
designer: Jonas Schudel, Azza Alameddine, Hanna Donker, Fabio Haag, Ben Jones, Samir Reis, José Solé
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Effra as a contemporary humanist sans tracing its heritage to the early sans letters of Caslon Junior, Red Dot Design Award winner, with clean lines and open shapes across extensive Weight and Italic variable axes.
summary: A multi-script humanist sans drawing on Caslon Junior's early sans letters, Red Dot Design Award winner, with extensive Weight and Italic variable axes.
description: A humanist sans led by Jonas Schudel and developed with Azza Alameddine, Hanna Donker, Fabio Haag, Ben Jones, Samir Reis and José Solé, tracing its heritage to the early sans letters of Caslon Junior. Nine weights run from Hairline to Black with matching italics, and a 2-axis variable font handles Weight and Italic. Multi-script coverage across Arabic, Cyrillic, Greek and Latin Extended and a Red Dot Design Award make it a serious workhorse for global brand systems, editorial and product work that wants a calm, open voice.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Effra as a contemporary humanist sans typeface and Red Dot Design Award winner, with a heritage traced back to the early sans letters of Caslon Junior. The family was designed by Jonas Schudel with Azza Alameddine, Hanna Donker, Fabio Haag, Ben Jones, Samir Reis and José Solé between 2008 and 2024, and offers clean lines and open shapes across extensive Weight and Italic axes.

name: Elevon
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Elevon as a distinctive sans typeface ready to head into space, with five conveniently named weights for diversity and contrast, drawn to give the right tone of voice to space and future-facing work.
summary: A geometric sans by Bruno Maag with five g-force weights from ZeroG to FourG, drawn for tech and future-facing branding across Cyrillic, Greek and Latin.
description: A geometric sans by Bruno Maag with a confident, future-facing voice. Five weights, named ZeroG to FourG, give the family diversity and contrast without the conventional Light to Black ladder. Multi-script coverage across Cyrillic, Greek and Latin Extended makes it a strong pick for tech, aerospace and brand work that wants a forward-looking tone without slipping into pure science fiction.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Elevon as a distinctive sans typeface that is ready to head into space, designed by Bruno Maag between 2011 and 2021. They highlight five conveniently named weights for diversity and contrast, framed as the right tone of voice for a moon mission.

name: Foco
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Foco as a friendly humanist sans, highly flexible at both display and text sizes, with brush-inspired details that make it easily recognisable for branding work.
summary: A friendly multi-script humanist sans by Fabio Haag with brush-inspired details, drawn to flex between display and text sizes for branding work.
description: A humanist sans by Fabio Haag with brush-inspired details that lend warmth and a recognisable voice. Four weights run Light, Regular, Bold and Black with matching italics, holding up at large sizes for headline work and remaining readable at text sizes. Multi-script coverage across Cyrillic, Greek and Latin Extended makes it a useful pick for international brand and editorial systems that need character without losing legibility.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Foco as a friendly humanist sans typeface, designed by Fabio Haag between 2007 and 2023. They highlight the brush-inspired details that make the design easily recognisable and frame it as a good choice for branding, flexible across display and text sizes.

name: Fraise
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Fraise as a fresh and fruity humanist sans with a tall x-height and distinctive round shapes, inspired by the work of Roger Excoffon, with a Weight axis from body text to display.
summary: A humanist sans by Pablo Bosch inspired by Roger Excoffon, with a tall x-height, distinctive round shapes and a Weight axis that runs from text to display, awarded by Communication Arts.
description: A humanist sans by Pablo Bosch that nods to Roger Excoffon's mid-century work, with a tall x-height and distinctive round shapes that give the family its character. Ten weights run from Hairline to Ultra with matching italics, and a 2-axis variable font handles Weight and Italic. Suited to a wide stretch of brand and editorial work, from running text to confident display, and recognised with a Communication Arts Award of Excellence.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Fraise as a fresh and fruity humanist sans typeface with a tall x-height and distinctive round shapes, designed by Pablo Bosch in 2024 and inspired by the work of Roger Excoffon. They highlight the extensive Weight axis as making the family suitable for design and branding from body text to display, and note recognition with a Communication Arts Award of Excellence.

name: Gardez
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describes Gardez as a serif display font born from introducing inverted contrast into a bold, garalde-style italic, producing a highly expressive design.
summary: A bold garalde italic with inverted contrast, drawn for packaging, posters and book covers.
description: A serif display by Rafael Saraiva that fuses inverted contrast with a bold, garalde-style italic. Dalton Maag pitch it for packaging, logos, posters, magazines and book covers, where its expressive contrast does the heavy lifting. The italic-only construction makes it a specialist headline face rather than a workhorse, and the support is Latin only.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Gardez as an unusual and striking serif display font born from the unexpected introduction of inverted contrast into a bold, garalde-style italic. They frame the fusion as a highly expressive design suited to packaging, logos, posters, magazines and book covers. Designed by Rafael Saraiva and released in 2022.

name: Gelo
classification_notes: Dalton Maag positions Gelo as a hybrid that combines brush lettering elegance and playfulness with the structure and versatility of a slab-serif.
summary: A brush-meets-slab serif by Tom Foley and Selma Losch, expressive enough for packaging and editorial yet structured enough for text.
description: A hybrid serif by Tom Foley and Selma Losch that grafts the elegance of brush lettering onto a slab-serif skeleton. The mix gives Gelo an expressive headline voice while keeping the rhythm needed for branding, editorial, catalogues and children's books. Italics and a Latin character set round out a family Dalton Maag describes as flexible and warm.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Gelo as a typeface that combines the elegance and playfulness of brush lettering with the structure and versatility of a slab-serif, calling it expressive and flexible and pointing to packaging, branding, editorial, catalogues and children's books. Designed by Tom Foley and Selma Losch and released in 2017.

name: Grueber
classification_notes: Dalton Maag classifies Grueber as a robust slab-serif display typeface inspired by drawings of Austrian architect Paul Grueber, with quirky expressive alternates that amplify its tone.
summary: A robust slab-serif display by Lukas Paltram, drawn from Austrian architect Paul Grueber's lettering and pushed further by quirky alternates.
description: A slab-serif display by Lukas Paltram that takes its cues from drawings by Austrian architect Paul Grueber. Dalton Maag describes it as a compact family with a strong tone of voice, amplified by expressive alternates. Sits well on posters, packaging and identity work that wants architectural weight without losing personality.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Grueber as a robust, slab-serif display typeface inspired by the drawings of Austrian architect Paul Grueber. They highlight a compact family with a strong tone of voice in visual communication, further amplified by quirky expressive alternates. Designed by Lukas Paltram and released in 2025.

name: Haas Recast
classification_notes: Dalton Maag classifies Haas Recast as a revival of a classic faux-Swiss grotesque sans typeface originally designed in the 1950s by Max Miedinger and Eduard Hoffmann for the Haas Type Foundry, updated as a variable font with Weight, Width, Italic and Tracking axes.
summary: A variable-font revival of the 1950s Haas grotesque by Miedinger and Hoffmann, with Weight, Width, Italic and Tracking axes.
description: A grotesque sans by Eduardo Rennó that revives a 1950s Haas Type Foundry design by Max Miedinger and Eduard Hoffmann, the lineage that led to Helvetica. Dalton Maag's update is built as a variable font with Weight, Width, Italic and Tracking axes, which makes it more flexible than its forebear while keeping the calm Swiss neutrality that draws people to grotesques in the first place.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Haas Recast as a revival of a classic faux-Swiss grotesque sans typeface, originally designed in the 1950s by Max Miedinger and Eduard Hoffmann for the Haas Type Foundry. They highlight Weight, Width, Italic and Tracking axes in the variable font. Designed by Eduardo Rennó and released in 2025.

name: Highgate
classification_notes: Dalton Maag classifies Highgate as a contemporary incised typeface inspired by forms seen in British stone carving, with Weight and Italic axes plus a Chisel axis for extra expression. Awarded a Communication Arts Award of Excellence.
summary: A contemporary incised serif drawn from British stone carving, with a Chisel axis that lets the cut deepen on demand.
description: An incised typeface by Pablo Gámez and Elena Peralta with open shapes, varied proportions and a flexible, friendly tone. The reference is British stone carving and the variable font carries Weight and Italic axes plus a Chisel axis that intensifies the cut for expressive headlines. The family won a Communication Arts Award of Excellence and works across editorial, identity and culture briefs.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Highgate as a contemporary incised typeface with open shapes and varied proportions for flexible, friendly and sophisticated expression. They cite British stone carving as inspiration and call out Weight, Italic and Chisel axes. Designed by Pablo Gámez and Elena Peralta, released in 2024, awarded a Communication Arts Award of Excellence.

name: InterFace
designer: Bruno Maag, Naïma Ben Ayed, Carlos De Toro, Angel Kwong
classification_notes: Dalton Maag classifies InterFace as a sans typeface that combines humanist, grotesque and industrial features, with extensive weight range and a track record across signage, billboards, text and small print.
summary: A hybrid sans by Bruno Maag and team that blends humanist, grotesque and industrial cues, with Arabic, Cyrillic, Greek and Latin support.
description: A sans by Bruno Maag with Naïma Ben Ayed, Carlos De Toro and Angel Kwong that mixes humanist warmth, grotesque rhythm and industrial structure. Dalton Maag points to a long track record from signage and billboards down to text and small print. With Arabic, Cyrillic, Greek, Latin and Latin Extended, it suits multilingual identity systems and infrastructure-scale signage.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe InterFace as a unique sans typeface that combines humanist, grotesque and industrial features, with an extensive weight range and a proven track record across signage, billboards, text and small print. Designed by Bruno Maag, Naïma Ben Ayed, Carlos De Toro and Angel Kwong, released in 2025.

name: Jazzier
classification_notes: Dalton Maag classifies Jazzier as a caricature of an oldstyle serif typeface, packed with typographic surprises and unexpected charm.
summary: An oldstyle serif played for surprise, drawn for book covers, posters, merchandise and packaging.
description: A display serif by Ignacio Casco Guijarro that reads like a caricature of an oldstyle, leaning into typographic surprises and unexpected charm. Dalton Maag pitch it at book covers, posters, merchandise and packaging, where the family's character does more work than a neutral serif could. Best at display sizes.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Jazzier as a caricature of an oldstyle serif typeface, packed with typographic surprises and unexpected charm. They recommend it for book cover design, posters, merchandise and packaging. Designed by Ignacio Casco Guijarro and released in 2021.

name: Jovie
classification_notes: Dalton Maag classifies Jovie as a delicious soft-serif typeface with a versatile variable font, featuring an extensive Weight axis, playful Italics, swashes, ligatures, expressive alternates and an eye-catching Glow style.
summary: A soft-serif by Franziska Hubmann with playful italics, swashes and a Glow display style for brand systems that want warmth.
description: A soft-serif by Franziska Hubmann that rounds the contrast and corners of a traditional serif into something warmer. The variable font runs an extensive Weight axis with playful italics, swashes, ligatures and expressive alternates, and the Glow style is built for headline impact. Sits comfortably across text and display in identity, editorial and packaging.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Jovie as a delicious soft-serif typeface with a versatile variable font, citing an extensive Weight axis, playful Italics, swashes, ligatures, expressive alternates and an eye-catching Glow style. They say it shines in text and display, offering warmth and elegance. Designed by Franziska Hubmann and released in 2024.

name: King's Caslon
classification_notes: Dalton Maag classifies King's Caslon as an elegant revival of the Caslon typeface tradition, with four text styles and two display styles all carrying the family's iconic ampersand.
summary: An elegant Caslon revival by Veronika Burian and Ron Carpenter, with text and display cuts and a much-loved ampersand.
description: A Caslon revival by Veronika Burian and Ron Carpenter that splits into four text styles and two display styles. Dalton Maag describes the family as joyously elegant, and the ampersand that runs through every cut has become its calling card. Reach for it on classical editorial, luxury identity and book work where you want Caslon's authority with contemporary drawing.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe King's Caslon as a joyously elegant revival of the Caslon typeface tradition, with four text styles and two display styles, all featuring the family's now iconic ampersand and bringing classical sophistication to any project. Designed by Veronika Burian and Ron Carpenter, released in 2019.

name: Lemance
classification_notes: Dalton Maag classifies Lemance as a humanist sans typeface inspired by its designer's research into the calligraphic origins of italics and their influence on typography.
summary: A humanist sans drawn from research into the calligraphic origins of italics, with vibrant energy across upright and italic cuts.
description: A humanist sans by Damien Collot, drawn from his research into the calligraphic origins of italics and how that lineage has shaped typography. The result is a family that reads as elegant and characterful, with italics that carry real handwriting energy rather than slanted uprights. Suits editorial, culture and identity briefs that want warmth without going fully into script.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Lemance as a humanist sans typeface inspired by its designer's research into the calligraphic origins of italics and their influence on typography, calling the family elegant and characterful with vibrant energy. Designed by Damien Collot and released in 2017.

name: Letter Gothic Recast
designer: Samir Reis, Emanuela Conidi, Bruno Mello, Natalia Qadreh
classification_notes: Dalton Maag classifies Letter Gothic Recast as a revival of the classic humanist sans typewriter typeface originally designed by Roger Roberson for IBM in the 1950s, fully updated as a monospace variable font with Slant and Weight axes from Hairline to Black.
summary: A variable-font revival of Roger Roberson's IBM Letter Gothic, now monospace with Slant and Weight axes from Hairline to Black.
description: A monospace revival by Samir Reis, Emanuela Conidi, Bruno Mello and Natalia Qadreh of the humanist typewriter face Roger Roberson drew for IBM in the 1950s. Dalton Maag's update keeps the typewriter character but turns it into a variable font with a Slant axis and a Weight axis running from Hairline to Black. Sits well in code, technical typography, editorial captions and identity systems wanting machine-age authenticity with modern range.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Letter Gothic Recast as their revival of the classic humanist sans typewriter typeface, originally designed by Roger Roberson for IBM in the 1950s. They highlight a fully updated monospace variable font with a Slant axis and a Weight axis from Hairline to Black. Designed by Samir Reis, Emanuela Conidi, Bruno Mello and Natalia Qadreh, released in 2025.

name: Lexia
designer: Ron Carpenter, Ignacio Casco Guijarro, Reiko Hirai, Franziska Hubmann, Robert Pratley
classification_notes: Dalton Maag classifies Lexia as a contemporary humanist slab-serif with a warm, friendly, inviting tone, supported by Weight and Italic variable font axes. Pairs with Lexia Mono.
summary: A humanist slab-serif with warmth in text and punch at heavier weights, pairing with Lexia Mono.
description: A humanist slab-serif drawn by Ron Carpenter, Ignacio Casco Guijarro, Reiko Hirai, Franziska Hubmann and Robert Pratley. Lexia keeps a warm, friendly tone in text and steps up to high-impact communication as the weight axis travels into Bold and Black. Cyrillic, Greek, Hebrew, Latin and Latin Extended make it a multilingual workhorse, and it pairs with Lexia Mono for systems that need a matching code face.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Lexia as a contemporary humanist slab-serif typeface with a warm, friendly, inviting tone, even when conveying a serious message. They highlight Weight and Italic variable font axes and list it as pairing with Lexia Mono. Designed by Ron Carpenter, Ignacio Casco Guijarro, Reiko Hirai, Franziska Hubmann and Robert Pratley, released in 2024.

name: Lexia Mono
designer: Ron Carpenter, Ignacio Casco Guijarro, Elí Castellanos, Spike Spondike
classification_notes: Dalton Maag classifies Lexia Mono as a monospace humanist slab-serif crafted for coders and designers working with tabular layouts, with extensive Weight and Italic variable font axes. Pairs with Lexia.
summary: A humanist slab-serif monospace built for code and tabular layouts, pairing directly with Lexia.
description: A monospace slab-serif by Ron Carpenter, Ignacio Casco Guijarro, Elí Castellanos and Spike Spondike that brings Lexia's humanist warmth to fixed-width settings. Dalton Maag describes the rhythm and even colour as remarkably easy to read, with Weight and Italic axes in the variable font. Pairs with Lexia for design systems that need text and code to share a voice.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Lexia Mono as a monospace humanist slab-serif typeface crafted for coders and designers working with tough tabular layouts. They cite consistent rhythm, even colour and extensive Weight and Italic axes in the variable font, and list it as pairing with Lexia. Designed by Ron Carpenter, Ignacio Casco Guijarro, Elí Castellanos and Spike Spondike, released in 2024.

name: Linzer
classification_notes: Dalton Maag classifies Linzer as a wholesome, creamy script font defined by smooth monoline curves, swashes and generous loops, with even strokes and rounded terminals.
summary: A monoline script by Franziska Hubmann inspired by the Austrian linzer cookie, with swashes and rounded terminals for packaging and branding.
description: A monoline script by Franziska Hubmann that lifts its name and feel from the classic Austrian linzer cookie. Smooth even strokes, swashes and generous loops give it a wholesome, creamy character that suits packaging, food and lifestyle branding. A specialist display script rather than a workhorse text family.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Linzer as a wholesome, creamy script font inspired by the classic Austrian cookie. They describe smooth monoline curves, swashes and generous loops, and even strokes with rounded terminals that bring a bakery-fresh appeal to packaging and branding. Designed by Franziska Hubmann and released in 2026.

name: Lubalin Recast Sans
classification_notes: Dalton Maag classifies Lubalin Recast Sans as a modern revival of a classic geometric sans typeface originally designed by Herb Lubalin in the 1970s from a magazine logo, with an extensive Weight axis and restored ligatures and alternates. Pairs with Lubalin Recast Serif.
summary: A revival of Herb Lubalin's 1970s geometric sans, with restored ligatures and alternates and a wide Weight axis.
description: A geometric sans by Yingtong Tan and Angel Kwong reviving the typeface Herb Lubalin drew in the 1970s from a magazine logo. Dalton Maag's update widens the Weight axis and brings back the unusual ligatures and alternates that gave the original its voice. Pairs with Lubalin Recast Serif for editorial systems that want the full Lubalin family back in service.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Lubalin Recast Sans as a modern revival of a classic geometric sans typeface, originally designed by Herb Lubalin in the 1970s from a magazine logo. They highlight an extensive Weight axis and the restoration of the design's unusual ligatures and alternates, and list it as pairing with Lubalin Recast Serif. Designed by Yingtong Tan and Angel Kwong, released in 2024.

name: Lubalin Recast Serif
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Lubalin Recast Serif as a fresh take on a classic geometric slab serif designed in the 1970s by Herb Lubalin to complement his existing sans, updated with an extensive Weight axis and the original's unusual ligatures and alternates.
summary: A geometric slab serif by Yingtong Tan and Angel Kwong, recasting Herb Lubalin's 1970s slab as a partner to Lubalin Recast Sans.
description: A geometric slab serif by Yingtong Tan and Angel Kwong that revives Herb Lubalin's 1970s slab and partners it with Lubalin Recast Sans. The variable font carries a Weight axis from Hairline to Black with matching italics, plus a Tracking axis and the family's signature ligatures, swash alternates and forwards and backwards slants. Reach for it on editorial covers, fashion mastheads, packaging and posters where you want the warmth and quirk of seventies American display type.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Lubalin Recast Serif as their fresh take on a classic geometric slab serif typeface, designed in the 1970s by Herb Lubalin to complement his existing sans. Their update includes an extensive Weight axis and matches the Sans's unusual ligatures and alternates.

name: Magpie
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Magpie as a faithful and elegant serif offering a modern view of history, warm with a cursive flow and built specifically for long-form text setting such as books.
summary: A warm, cursive text serif by Vincent Connare for long-form reading, offered in four classic book styles.
description: A text serif by Vincent Connare drawn for long-form reading, with a warm voice and cursive italics. The four-style family pairs Regular and Bold with their italics, supported by small caps, discretionary ligatures, oldstyle and lining numerals, and alternates for q, R, V and p. Best at body sizes in books, journals and editorial layouts where you want a literary tone with a modern view of history.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Magpie as a faithful and elegant serif typeface that offers a modern view of history. Warm, with a cursive flow, they say it is designed specifically for long-form text setting such as books, with a four-style family providing distinguished expression in any project.

name: Marble Arch
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Marble Arch as a serif that embodies the grandeur and timeless elegance of its iconic London landmark namesake, with Weight, Italic and Optical Size axes spanning Caption, Text, SubHead, Headline and Poster sizes.
summary: A high-contrast modern serif by Carlos De Toro with Caption, Text, SubHead, Headline and Poster optical sizes, named for the London landmark.
description: A modern serif by Carlos De Toro with vertical stress, high contrast and teardrop terminals. The variable font runs Weight from Hairline to Black with true italics, and the Optical Size axis covers Caption, Text, SubHead, Headline and Poster cuts so a single system can serve everything from footnotes to billboards. Sits at home on luxury, fashion and editorial work that wants the calm grandeur of a London serif.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Marble Arch as a serif typeface which embodies the grandeur and timeless elegance of its iconic London landmark namesake. Its variable font has Weight, Italic and Optical Size axes, adapting to caption, text, headline or display regardless of scale.

name: Mendl Sans
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Mendl Sans as a humanist sans embodying Art Deco elegance with a fresh twist, with two tonal variants: Dawn (overtly geometric with quirky period features) and Dusk (more organic and approachable).
summary: A humanist Art Deco sans by Angel Kwong with Dawn and Dusk variants, the first overtly geometric, the second more organic.
description: A humanist sans by Angel Kwong drawn with Art Deco elegance and a contemporary edge. The variable font runs Weight from Hairline to Black with matching italics and offers two tonal variants: Dawn leans into geometric construction with quirky period features, while Dusk softens into more organic, approachable shapes. Pairs with Mendl Serif for editorial, fashion and hospitality work that wants a 1920s accent without pastiche.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Mendl Sans as a humanist sans typeface which embodies Art Deco elegance with a fresh twist. Its variable font has an extensive Weight axis and two tonal variants: Dawn is overtly geometric with some quirky period features, while Dusk is more organic and approachable.

name: Mendl Serif
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Mendl Serif as a spur serif that exudes Art Deco sophistication with a modern flair, with Weight axis and Dawn and Dusk tonal variants matching Mendl Sans.
summary: A spur serif by Angel Kwong, partner to Mendl Sans, with Dawn and Dusk variants and delicate decorative serifs.
description: A spur serif by Angel Kwong, drawn alongside Mendl Sans and sharing its Art Deco DNA. The variable font runs Weight from Hairline to Black with matching italics, with Dawn taking the geometric, period-quirky route and Dusk reading as more organic and approachable. Delicate decorative serifs, looped 2 and a low centre of gravity make it a natural for poetry, packaging and editorial features that want the period without the cliché.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Mendl Serif as a spur serif typeface that exudes Art Deco sophistication with a modern flair. Its variable font has an extensive Weight axis and two tonal variants: Dawn is overtly geometric with some quirky period features, while Dusk is more organic and approachable.

name: Minetto
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Minetto as a contemporary take on the effortlessly elegant broad-nib display scripts that dominated commercial design in the 1930s, unashamedly retro but designed to satisfy modern users with an extensive Weight axis.
summary: A broad-nib display script by Radek Lukasiewicz that revives 1930s commercial scripts and runs across a full Weight axis.
description: A broad-nib display script by Radek Lukasiewicz that channels the elegant commercial scripts of the 1930s with a calligraphic flair. The variable font runs Weight from Hairline to Black, supported by swash capitals, period-style alternates and a short x-height. Built for all-caps titling, packaging, mastheads and posters where you want unashamed retro charm with the discipline of a contemporary type system.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Minetto as their contemporary take on the effortlessly elegant broad-nib display script typefaces that dominated commercial design in the 1930s. Unashamedly retro, but designed to satisfy modern users, its variable font surprises with an extensive Weight axis.

name: Mokoko
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Mokoko as a contemporary geometric slab serif with Weight and Italic variable font axes, transitioning along its axes from clean and understated to bold and quirky.
summary: A contemporary geometric slab serif by Naima Ben Ayed, moving from clean and understated to bold and quirky along its axes.
description: A geometric slab serif by Naima Ben Ayed, built around open round forms, rectangular slabs and circular counters. The variable font runs Weight from Hairline to Black with oblique italics, and a flamboyant alternate Q sits alongside ff and ft ligatures for moments of personality. Holds up across branding, packaging and editorial systems that want a confident industrial slab without losing warmth.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Mokoko as a contemporary geometric slab-serif typeface. With Weight and Italic variable font axes, they say Mokoko is highly functional and easily transitions from clean and understated to bold and quirky along its axes, offering a wide range of expression.

name: Monte Stella
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Monte Stella as a constructed, modular display sans with a purposeful naivety and deliberate imperfection, inspired by the vernacular lettering of Milan, with Weight and Slant axes including an extreme Turbo Italic.
summary: A modular display sans by Riccardo De Franceschi rooted in Milanese vernacular lettering, with an extreme Turbo Italic.
description: A constructed display sans by Riccardo De Franceschi, drawn from the vernacular lettering of Milan with industrial, angular forms and triangular terminals. The variable font carries a Weight axis from Light to Heavy and a Slant axis that includes an extreme Turbo Italic for high-impact moments, plus a set of Milanese pictograms. Aimed at posters, identities and editorial features that want a city-specific accent rather than another neutral grotesque.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Monte Stella as a constructed, modular display sans typeface, with a purposeful naivety and deliberate imperfection. Inspired by the vernacular lettering of Milan, its variable font features Weight and Slant axes, including an extreme Turbo Italic for added impact.

name: Neumond
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Neumond as a conceptual typeface that plays with the limits of legible type design, reducing serif and line weights to the absolute minimum to deliver a sharp, precise geometric display font.
summary: A high-contrast geometric display by Lukas Paltram and Samir Reis, reducing serifs and line weights to the edge of legibility.
description: A geometric display by Lukas Paltram and Samir Reis that pushes serif and line weight to the edge of legibility. Gossamer thin strokes, spiky spurs, streamlined geometry and stylish alternates give it a sharp, precise voice, with a denser cut for moments that need more presence. Built for fashion editorial, beauty packaging, posters and titling where elegance and attitude matter more than text-size comfort.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Neumond as a conceptual typeface that plays with the limits of legible type design. They say it reduces serif and line weights to the absolute minimum, delivering a geometric display font which is sharp and precise, highly impactful and truly elegant.

name: Nine Elms
designer: Eduardo Renno, Franziska Hubmann, Natalia Qadreh
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Nine Elms as their new interpretation of the classic 1930s transitional serif newspaper typeface designed by Victor Lardent and Stanley Morison, with Roman and Italic variable fonts featuring Optical Size and extensive Weight axes.
summary: A transitional newspaper serif by Eduardo Renno, Franziska Hubmann and Natalia Qadreh, reinterpreting the Lardent and Morison Times design with optical sizes.
description: A transitional serif by Eduardo Renno, Franziska Hubmann and Natalia Qadreh that reinterprets the 1930s Times newspaper design by Victor Lardent and Stanley Morison. Roman and italic variable fonts each run a Weight axis from Hairline to Black plus Caption, Text, SubHead and Headline optical sizes, with small caps, oldstyle and lining numerals, ligatures and teardrop terminals. Built for editorial systems, newspapers and publishers wanting a Times-family voice with multilingual Cyrillic and Greek support.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Nine Elms as their new interpretation of the classic 1930s transitional serif newspaper typeface designed by Victor Lardent and Stanley Morison, updated for modern times. Its Roman and Italic variable fonts both feature Optical Size and extensive Weight axes.

name: Objektiv
designer: Bruno Mello, Sahar Afshar, Carlos De Toro, Hanna Donker, Maria Fontenelle, Pablo Gamez, Ben Jones, Jonny Pinhorn
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Objektiv as a clean geometric sans with unique human appeal, with extensive Weight and Italic variable axes and three stylistic variants (Mk1, Mk2 and Mk3) for flexibility across applications and media.
summary: A clean geometric sans system by a Dalton Maag team, with Mk1, Mk2 and Mk3 stylistic variants and ten writing systems.
description: A geometric sans system drawn by a team of Dalton Maag designers including Bruno Mello, Sahar Afshar, Carlos De Toro and Jonny Pinhorn. Three stylistic variants (Mk1, Mk2 and Mk3) swap alternates wholesale for adaptable humanism, classically geometric rounds, low-contrast strokes and blunt vertices. The variable font runs Weight from Hairline to Black with slanted italics, and the family supports ten writing systems including Arabic, Cyrillic, Devanagari, Greek, Hebrew and Thai. Built for branding, ui and signage at scale.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Objektiv as a clean geometric sans typeface with unique human appeal. With extensive Weight and Italic variable font axes, and three stylistic variants (Mk1, Mk2 and Mk3), they say it offers flexibility and adaptability across diverse applications and media.

name: Oscine
designer: Bruno Maag, Deiverson Ribeiro, Rafael Saraiva
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Oscine as a sans typeface featuring an unusual lowercase paired with more conventional capitals, with distinctive square shapes softened by rounded corners.
summary: A soft square sans by Bruno Maag, Deiverson Ribeiro and Rafael Saraiva, pairing an unusual lowercase with more conventional capitals.
description: A soft square sans by Bruno Maag, Deiverson Ribeiro and Rafael Saraiva. The lowercase carries a distinctive spurless construction with softened straight terminals, while the capitals stay closer to the convention so the family reads as friendly without losing structure. Eight styles run from Light to XBold with true italics, supported across Cyrillic, Greek and Latin. Suits children's brands, hospitality and consumer identities that want warmth with a modular accent.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Oscine as a sans typeface featuring an unusual lowercase paired with more conventional capitals. They note its distinctive square shapes are softened by rounded corners, adding a layer of friendliness, with the entire font family offering flexibility for various display settings.

name: Perenelle Sans
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Perenelle Sans as a modern humanist sans inspired by the distinctly British designs of the early 1900s, with generous proportions ensuring legibility with an approachable tone.
summary: A humanist sans by Robert Pratley drawing on early 1900s British lettering, paired with Perenelle Serif and offered across nine weights with italics.
description: A humanist sans by Robert Pratley with shapes shared with Perenelle Serif. Nine weights from Hairline to Black with matching italics and an XBold cut, supported by Weight, Width and Italic variable axes. The drawing carries vertical stress, characteristic numerals, expressive symbols and an alternate hairline r, all rooted in the distinctly British humanism of the early twentieth century. Pairs naturally with Perenelle Serif for editorial systems and brand voices wanting warmth with British poise.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Perenelle Sans as a modern humanist sans typeface inspired by the distinctly British designs of the early 1900s. Its generous proportions ensure legibility with an approachable tone, and its variable font offers extensive Weight, Width and Italic axes for exceptional versatility.

name: Perenelle Serif
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Perenelle Serif as a contemporary serif designed to support and complement its Sans cousin, with open letter shapes ensuring legibility and a warm, dignified tone, supported by extensive Weight, Width and Italic axes.
summary: A warm, dignified serif by Robert Pratley designed to partner Perenelle Sans, across nine weights with italics.
description: A contemporary serif by Robert Pratley, drawn to partner Perenelle Sans. Nine weights from Hairline to Black with matching italics and an XBold cut, supported by Weight, Width and Italic variable axes. Vertical stress, sharp-cut terminals, characteristic numerals and stacked fractions give it a warm, dignified tone for editorial body, branding and longform reading. Shares shapes with Perenelle Sans, so the two function as one system.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Perenelle Serif as a contemporary serif typeface designed to support and complement its Sans cousin. They say its open letter shapes ensure legibility with a warm and dignified tone, and its variable font offers extensive Weight, Width and Italic axes for exceptional versatility.

name: Plume
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Plume as a flowing serif initially inspired by the elegance of early italic typewriter fonts, combining beauty and functionality with high legibility and supremely expressive forms.
summary: An italic-led serif by Bruno Maag inspired by early italic typewriter fonts, with a Text family and a more expressive Advertising cut.
description: A serif by Bruno Maag rooted in the italic typewriter typefaces of the early twentieth century. Six styles run across Regular and Bold with true italics, plus an Advertising cut with adapted shapes for headline use. Whimsical shaping, unusual serif placement, prominent diacritics and playful outstrokes give it an editorial voice that leans into character rather than neutrality. Suits literary editorial, packaging and titling that want warmth and personality.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Plume as a flowing serif typeface, initially inspired by the elegance of early italic typewriter fonts. They say its modern design aesthetic combines beauty and functionality, creating a flexible font family that is both highly legible and supremely expressive.

name: Pranzo
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Pranzo as the ultimate comic book typeface, with three variable axes (Weight, Width, Slant) and a library of comic essentials including symbols, frames, speech balloons and thought bubbles available across every weight and style.
summary: A comic book display sans by Samar Zureik and Hanna Donker with three variable axes and built-in symbols, frames and speech balloons.
description: A display sans by Samar Zureik and Hanna Donker built explicitly for comic book typography. Three variable axes (Weight from Hairline to Black, Width from ExtraCondensed to ExtraExtended, and a Slant axis covering Italic, Upright and Backslant) combine with a kit of comic essentials, including frames, speech balloons, thought bubbles and onomatopoeic symbols, available across every style. Reach for it on covers, panels, posters and identity work that wants graphic-novel energy without bolting on extra assets.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Pranzo as the ultimate comic book typeface, with three huge variable font axes that cover every mood and moment. They highlight that it is packed with comic book essentials such as symbols, frames, speech balloons and thought bubbles, available across every weight and style.

name: Prometo
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Prometo as an instantly recognisable square sans with an industrial feel, drawn as if precision-engineered, with an extensive weight range intended for display use across signage, titles and billboards.
summary: A square industrial sans by Fabio Haag and Fernando Caro built for signage, titles and billboards, with seven weights from Thin to Black plus italics.
description: A square industrial sans by Fabio Haag and Fernando Caro that reads as precision-engineered hardware. Fourteen styles run from Thin through Black with matching italics, drawn for display use at scale on signage, titles and billboards. The straight-sided counters and clipped terminals push it towards engineering, automotive and technology contexts where you want a precise, mechanical voice without falling into a generic geometric.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Prometo as an instantly recognisable square sans typeface with an industrial feel that looks like it has been constructed with precision. They note its extensive weight range is intended for display use, working well across signage, titles and billboards.

name: Setimo
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Setimo as a compact humanist sans that delivers a distinguished and authoritative tone, with a versatile family that helps readers navigate, absorb and understand a message.
summary: A compact humanist sans by Fernando Caro tuned for clear hierarchy and readable longform support across three weights with italics.
description: A compact humanist sans by Fernando Caro that aims for a distinguished, authoritative tone without crowding the page. Six styles cover Light, Regular and Bold with matching italics, with proportions drawn so the family acts as a quiet support to surrounding design rather than competing with it. Reach for it in editorial systems, corporate identities and interfaces that want humanist warmth at smaller sizes.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Setimo as a compact humanist sans typeface that delivers a distinguished and authoritative tone. They frame the versatile font family as one that works cohesively to enhance surrounding design elements, aiding readers in navigating, absorbing and understanding the message.

name: Shader
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Shader as a sans typeface that celebrates esports with a proud gamer aesthetic, legible and performant on screen but expressive and distinctive in display settings, with Solid and Inline variable fonts on an extensive Weight axis.
summary: A gaming display sans by Rafael Saraiva with Solid and Inline variable styles across nine weights, tuned to read on stream and shout in headlines.
description: A display sans by Rafael Saraiva built around an esports brief. Eighteen styles split between Shader Solid and Shader Inline, each variable on weight from Hairline to Black, so the same family can hold ranking tables and team names at small sizes then push into chunky inline lockups for tournament keyart. The sharp angles and broadcast-friendly proportions slot into gaming identity, streaming overlays and tech editorial that wants a distinctly gamer voice.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Shader as a sans typeface which celebrates esports with a proud gamer aesthetic. They frame the family as legible and performant on screen but expressive and distinctive in display settings, with Solid and Inline variable fonts featuring an extensive Weight axis.

name: Shader Color
designer: Maria Fontenelle, Robert Pratley, Deiverson Ribeiro
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Shader Color as three groundbreaking typefaces that reimagine the existing Shader design for its colour-font debut, naming Shader 16 (16-bit pixels), Shader 64 (64-bit chrome) and Shader Glitch (a future where technology conspires against us).
summary: A trio of colour fonts by Maria Fontenelle, Robert Pratley and Deiverson Ribeiro that take the Shader gaming sans into 16-bit pixels, 64-bit chrome and glitch.
description: A colour-font extension of the Shader esports family by Maria Fontenelle, Robert Pratley and Deiverson Ribeiro. Three styles cover Shader 16 with bright 16-bit pixel artwork, Shader 64 in shiny 64-bit chrome and Shader Glitch with a corrupted, distorted treatment. Each style ships as a single colour font intended for posters, broadcast graphics, esports identity and any gaming context that wants ready-made texture rather than a layered build.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Shader Color as three groundbreaking typefaces that reimagine the existing Shader design for its colour-font debut. They cite Shader 16 with bright 16-bit pixels, Shader 64 with shiny 64-bit chrome and Shader Glitch as a future where technology conspires against us.

name: Soleto
designer: Fernando Caro, Fabio Haag, Rafael Saraiva
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Soleto as a contemporary sans family that exudes quiet confidence, excelling in longform text with even rhythm and texture, and combining traditional letter shapes with a modern, inviting appeal for branding and corporate use.
summary: A humanist sans by Fernando Caro, Fabio Haag and Rafael Saraiva with eighteen styles tuned for longform text and corporate identity.
description: A humanist sans by Fernando Caro, Fabio Haag and Rafael Saraiva that traded across a decade of work into a contemporary family with quiet confidence. Eighteen styles span Hairline to Black with matching italics, with an even rhythm and texture intended to hold longform text without falling out of voice in display use. The traditional letter shapes give it a warm, inviting register that suits branding, annual reports and corporate communications.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Soleto as a contemporary sans font family that exudes quiet confidence. They highlight its even rhythm and texture for longform text, and frame it as combining traditional letter shapes with a modern, inviting appeal that is perfect for branding and corporate use.

name: Stroudley
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Stroudley as a humanist sans originally designed for signage, with meticulously crafted structures that save space while ensuring optimal legibility, framed as versatile enough to handle a wide range of design challenges.
summary: A humanist signage sans by Bruno Maag and Ron Carpenter built to save space without losing legibility, in three weights with matching italics.
description: A humanist sans by Bruno Maag and Ron Carpenter that began as a signage commission and grew into a six-style family covering Light, Regular and Bold with matching italics. The structures are tightened to save horizontal space while keeping aperture and contrast tuned for legibility at distance and small size, so it can move from station boards into editorial, identity and interface work without rebuilding the type system.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Stroudley as a humanist sans typeface originally designed for signage. They highlight its meticulously crafted structures that save space while ensuring optimal legibility, and frame the versatile font family as one that can confidently tackle a wide range of design challenges.

name: Tareco
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Tareco as a lively, syncopated script inspired by the beloved Brazilian biscuit, with thick confident strokes and precise details that move with a jazz-like rhythm for packaging, posters and expressive headlines.
summary: A syncopated brush script by Deiverson Ribeiro inspired by Brazilian biscuit lettering, drawn for packaging, posters and expressive headlines.
