description: A single-weight brush script by Deiverson Ribeiro that takes its rhythm from the lettering on the beloved Brazilian Tareco biscuit. Confident XBold strokes meet precise details and an off-beat baseline that gives the family a jazz-like cadence. Designed for packaging, posters and expressive headlines where you want a loud, playful Brazilian voice rather than a polished anglophone script.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Tareco as a lively, syncopated script font inspired by the beloved Brazilian biscuit. They highlight thick, confident strokes and precise details that move with a jazz-like rhythm to bring a loud, playful personality to packaging, poster designs and expressive headlines.

name: Tephra
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Tephra as an ambitious display typeface offering an unusual approach to typography, with six styles designed to be layered with varying colours or effects so designers can create exceptional, one-of-a-kind results.
summary: A layered modular display by Hamish Muir with six styles built to stack in different colours and produce one-of-a-kind compositions.
description: A modular display family by Hamish Muir built as a layering system rather than a single typeface. Six styles, indexed Tephra 0, 12, 50, 100, 138 and 250, register against each other so designers can stack them in varying colours or effects to build bespoke compositions. Reach for it on posters, covers, identity work and any campaign where the typography itself needs to behave like a graphic system.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Tephra as an ambitious display typeface that offers designers an unusual approach to typography. They frame its six styles as designed to be layered with varying colours or effects, enabling designers to create exceptional, one-of-a-kind results.

name: Tondo
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Tondo as a sans typeface with rounded terminals that always makes an impact on screen or at display sizes, with a friendly personality suited to wayfinding, branding and advertising.
summary: A rounded sans by Veronika Burian with a dedicated Signage cut, used widely in wayfinding, branding and advertising.
description: A rounded sans by Veronika Burian whose soft terminals give it an immediate, friendly presence on screen and at display sizes. Four weights cover Light, Regular, Bold and a dedicated Signage cut tuned for wayfinding contexts. The family sits comfortably in advertising, branding, packaging and consumer products that want approachable warmth without sliding into a child-like rounded geometric.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Tondo as a sans typeface with rounded terminals that always makes an impact, whether on screen or at display sizes. They highlight the rounded font family's appealing personality, calling it suitable for a wide range of applications from wayfinding to branding and advertising.

name: Tornac
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Tornac as a handwritten script that feels like a warm summer afternoon, with a pleasing rhythmic flow that feels natural and spontaneous and broad proportions that exude elegance, suited to magazine titling, packaging and advertising.
summary: A casual handwritten script by Jeremie Hornus with a warm, rhythmic flow drawn for magazine titling, packaging and advertising.
description: A handwritten script by Jeremie Hornus drawn for magazine titling, packaging and advertising. A single Regular cut carries broad, generous proportions and a rhythmic, spontaneous flow that lands closer to a confident pen than a calligraphic exhibit. Reach for it where you want warmth, summer-light energy and a personal voice without breaking into ornament.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Tornac as a handwritten script font that feels like a warm summer afternoon. They frame its pleasing rhythmic flow as natural and spontaneous, and its broad proportions as elegant, calling it perfectly suited for magazine titling, packaging and advertising.

name: Venn
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Venn as a humanist sans designed for high performance in demanding environments, with open counters and clear shapes for legibility and Weight and Width axes in variable font format for flexibility.
summary: A humanist sans system by Fernando Caro and Deiverson Ribeiro with Weight and Width axes across five widths, tuned for demanding interface and signage work.
description: A humanist sans system by Fernando Caro and Deiverson Ribeiro built around two variable axes. Weight runs Light to XBold across five widths, from Condensed through SemiCondensed, Normal and SemiExtended to Extended, giving twenty-five static styles plus full variable coverage. Open counters and clear shapes prioritise legibility in demanding environments such as interfaces, dashboards, wayfinding and broadcast.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Venn as a humanist sans typeface designed for high performance in the most demanding environments. They highlight its open counters and clear shapes for enhanced legibility, and its Weight and Width axes in variable font format for flexibility and adaptability.

name: Verveine
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Verveine as a charming and authentic informal handwritten script suitable across a range of media, created as a commercial extension of the freeware font Trashhand, with a completely new lowercase design and enhanced writing system support.
summary: An informal handwritten script by Luce Avérous, a commercial extension of the freeware Trashhand with a new lowercase and broader language support.
description: An informal handwritten script by Luce Avérous developed as a commercial extension of the popular freeware font Trashhand. The lowercase has been redrawn from scratch and the writing system support significantly expanded, while the family keeps the spontaneous marker-pen energy that made the original a default for casual social, packaging and editorial work. A single Regular cut carries the family.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Verveine as a charming and authentic informal handwritten script font suitable for use across a range of media. They frame it as a commercial extension of the freeware font Trashhand, with a completely new lowercase design and enhanced writing system support.

name: Volina
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Volina as an enchanting script with generous ascenders and descenders and elaborate capitals, inspired by the movement of ribbons, with a 3D effect drawn from a thin channel of negative space that produces a captivating optical rhythm and a sophisticated, delicate feel.
summary: A ribbon-inspired display script by Francesca Bolognini and Selma Losch with generous extenders and a thin negative-space channel that gives a 3D feel.
description: A display script by Francesca Bolognini and Selma Losch whose forms read as folded ribbons. A single Regular cut carries generous ascenders and descenders, elaborate capitals and a thin channel of negative space that produces a 3D effect across the strokes. The optical rhythm is delicate and sophisticated, suited to fashion, luxury packaging, magazine titling and editorial work that wants a couture script voice.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Volina as an enchanting script font with generous ascenders and descenders and elaborate capitals. They frame it as inspired by the movement of ribbons, with a 3D effect drawn from a thin channel of negative space that generates a captivating optical rhythm and a sophisticated, delicate feel.

name: Widescreen
classification_notes: Dalton Maag describe Widescreen as a seemingly normal geometric sans concealing an alter ego of an ultra-wide, brutalist design at the opposite end of its variable Width axis, with Weight and Mixed axes that allow maximum distortion mixing normal and ultra-wide.
summary: A geometric to brutalist variable sans by Matt Burvill that travels from a normal width to an ultra-wide alter ego, with a Mixed axis for distortion.
description: A variable sans by Matt Burvill that hides a brutalist alter ego inside a polite geometric. Twenty-seven styles spread across three widths (Normal, Extended and UltraExtended) at nine weights from Hairline to Black, with a Mixed axis that lets letters distort across the Width range so a single word can move from upright geometric to ultra-wide brutalism. Reach for it on covers, posters, identity work and campaigns that want typographic transformation as part of the message.
foundry_blurb: Dalton Maag describe Widescreen as a seemingly normal geometric sans typeface that conceals an alter ego: an ultra-wide, brutalist design at the opposite end of its variable font Width axis. They note that its Weight and Mixed axes allow for maximum distortion, mixing normal and ultra-wide.
